findings: Live cron state proves job c99cfa5f-41a1-4bc2-845f-6b6e1d5c7e80 is real and enabled on schedule '*/10 * * * *' in Europe/Berlin, not just a config stub.

The latest persisted cron state shows the last run started at 2026-05-20 20:40:12 UTC and ended in error after 600317 ms with lastDiagnosticSummary 'cron: job execution timed out' and lastDeliveryStatus 'unknown'.

The run-history file ~/.openclaw/cron/runs/c99cfa5f-41a1-4bc2-845f-6b6e1d5c7e80.jsonl contains only a timeout finish event for that run; there is no successful delivery event or durable success report in cron history.
The watchdog script exists and does execute the real path: checker/editorial/agentic, optional aggressive repair loop, verifier, status artifact writes, and self-delete only on healthy exit.

A safe forced run at 2026-05-20 20:52 UTC reproduced the real failure path: it ended with DOCS_STACK_STILL_BROKEN, did not self-delete, and rewrote docs_stack_temp_watchdog_status.json with healthy=false.

Current watchdog status after the forced run shows docsStateFingerprint 50d45152221f1acb, agentic status fail, shouldUserNeedToRepeatThis=true, and parallel-signoff fingerprint mismatch against the existing signoff a0b7bc42142e43c1.

Overlap/lock behavior is real but not robustly handled: runner and verifier returned exit 75 due to another Ralph docs loop holding the global lock, yet the watchdog still remained unhealthy and the surrounding cron job already timed out once.

Current docs-health signaling is coherent enough to show failure, but not healthy enough to approve the watchdog overall because reporting is not proving successful end-to-end delivery on schedule and the signoff model is invalidated by changing fingerprints.

blockers: The 10-minute cron job has already timed out in live operation, so end-to-end execution is not robust enough yet.

The watchdog's approval/signoff model is unstable because the docs-state fingerprint changes during normal artifact updates, invalidating an otherwise green signoff before the watchdog can declare health.
Lock-contention handling is incomplete in practice: runner/verifier can skip with exit 75, but the watchdog still fails and the cron layer can time out before a coherent success report is delivered.
Agentic health is still genuinely failing (shouldUserNeedToRepeatThis=true), so approval would be false even if reporting alone were working.
